content:
Claude Code spends ~50 of your ~200 reliable instructions before your CLAUDE.md loads. Here’s the ten-file harness that respects the rest.

5 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Frontier models hold reliable adherence to roughly 150–200 instructions. Claude Code’s own system prompt spends about 50 of them before your CLAUDE.md is even read.
That arithmetic is why I don’t buy the thirty-file agent-config starter. Everything that loads at session start — every rule file, every skill description, every agent name — sits in context on every single turn. A starter that installs thirty files isn’t a stronger harness. It just spends your instruction budget before your first prompt arrives.
So when I packaged my own harness as an installable starter, the constraint was the product: ten files, one command.
npx leanharnessZero dependencies, no prompts, no network calls, no postinstall step. Files that already exist are skipped and listed; overwriting anything takes an explicit --force. Every file in the set exists because of a rule I'd already published on my site — the starter just turns that rule set into an install command.
The harness decides more than the model…
